_meta.notes: Master machine-readable configuration for all tunables. Every field marked with 'tunable' must follow the safe_range contract before shipping. Changes require a ParameterPatch record in /patches/.

economy._comment: Core revenue and cost scaling formulas

tunables.provenance: Stardew Valley baseline — 5c for Wheat maps to ~1min active time. Scalar lets live-ops adjust without rewriting crop table.
tunables.notes: Values >3.0 cause prestige to unlock in <10min, breaking retention loop.

tunables.provenance: Cookie Clicker research: building multipliers plateau around 50x before players feel diminishing returns.
tunables.notes: Hard cap prevents hyperinflation past the T5 machine era.

tunables.provenance: 0.08 chosen after sim v1.0.2 to slightly boost early-game CPS scalar.
tunables.notes: Values >0.25 make first Auto Harvester feel like a mandatory purchase, hurting early retention.

tunables.provenance: 1.15 is the idle-game industry standard (AdVenture Capitalist, Realm Grinder). Below 1.08 players max all upgrades too fast; above 1.35 creates a 'wall' around level 5.
tunables.notes: Test in sim at 1.10, 1.15, 1.25 before changing.

tunables.provenance: Automation must feel premium vs farming but not gated until 30min in.
tunables.notes: Ratio farming:automation should stay near 1:3 for pacing.

tunables.provenance: 10 levels keeps ARPU curve smooth over a 20h session. More levels push monetization pressure.
tunables.notes: If you extend to 20, recalibrate base costs downward by ~30%.

tunables.provenance: 1.18 from Idle Miner Tycoon mine-shaft cost progression. Targets 10x cost between T1 and T5.
tunables.notes: At 1.10 all tiers feel cheap; at 1.30 T4+ machines are gated behind prestige which breaks Roblox-flow.

tunables.provenance: Each tier should double base CPS contribution for clear power fantasy.
tunables.notes: Never let T5 be >100x T1 or economy inflates past prestige formula ceiling.

tunables.provenance: Unlock thresholds set relative to expected coins at each progression stage. Sim validates.
tunables.notes: Reduce to 0.7 during new-player campaign; revert after 30 days.

tunables.provenance: 0.12 means 10-level automation = 2.2x income — matches Factorio belt-chain satisfaction target.
tunables.notes: Values >0.20 make idle income too dominant; players stop actively playing.

tunables.provenance: Lowered from 6 to 4 after SimulationQA reported p50 automation took 72min. Goal is 40min.
tunables.notes: Setting to 1 makes the game fully idle immediately. Setting to 10 gates it behind mid-game.

tunables.provenance: Max 80% of income should come from idle sources. Preserves active play loop.
tunables.notes: Above 0.95 players close the tab and return hours later, destroying session length metrics.

tunables.provenance: Raised to 8M after SimulationQA showed Casual players rushing prestige 96% of the time. 8M forces longer sessions before reset.
tunables.notes: Lowering to 500k speeds prestige but may cause players to reset before experiencing industrial farming.

tunables.provenance: 0.02 → 10 prestiges = 20% machine efficiency bonus. Keeps prestige feeling meaningful without trivialing content.
tunables.notes: Cap enforced separately at 50%; changing slope without adjusting cap can break high-prestige runs.

tunables.provenance: 1 prestige point = 1 gem. Gems are cosmetic-only currency. Ratio controls gem inflation.
tunables.notes: Do NOT lower below 0.5 — gems buy QOL items only (not power); this is our P2W firewall.

weather._comment: Dynamic weather events affecting crop growth and yields

events.notes: Baseline state. Weight should remain dominant to avoid 'every event is special' fatigue.
growth_speed_mod=3.0 causes visual overlap of multiple harvests in one tick — test in sim.
Never drop yield_mod below 0.3 — playtesters report frustration that breaks session at that level.
This is a positive event — weight should be lower than sunny to preserve perceived rarity.

events.default_en: Pest Attack! Crop yield severely reduced for 30s.
events.notes: Short duration is intentional — punishing but recoverable. Weight >15 creates hostile game feel.

tunables.provenance: 0.05 per level → max level 10 worker provides +50% bonus. Mirrors RPG stat curves.
tunables.notes: Values >0.15 make worker leveling feel better than building machines — breaks machine economy.

tunables.provenance: Exponential level-up cost prevents workers from being a coin sink with infinite returns.
tunables.notes: At 2.5, leveling past Lv5 becomes prohibitively expensive for mid-game; cap at Lv10 in that case.

tunables.provenance: hire_cost / base_bonus should be ~2000 coins per 1% bonus. Comparable to Upgrade IV purchase.
tunables.notes: If ratio <500, workers outvalue upgrades entirely. Rebalance both in tandem.

value_multiplier_per_stage.provenance: 2.3x from Factorio beacon math: each productive module costs ~40s of throughput delay for 2.3x output. Justified by processing machine cost.
value_multiplier_per_stage.notes: Values >4.0 make processed goods so valuable that raw crop selling becomes obsolete entirely.

max_stages.provenance: 4 stages (raw → processed → refined → luxury → export) mirrors manufacturing games. Beyond 6 becomes cognitively overwhelming for casual players.
max_stages.notes: Each stage needs a dedicated processing machine. Adding stages requires adding machines first.
